channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: OpenAI's Altman will donate $1 million to Trump's inaugural fund
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is planning to make a $1 million personal donation to President-Elect Donald Trump's inauguration fund, joining a number of tech companies and executives who are working to improve their relationships the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to write a holiday rom-com for TV, according to the experts
item.description: Have you ever watched a holiday rom-com on Hallmark Channel or Lifetime — or any of the other many networks and streamers that now air them — and thought, “I could write that”? It's harder than you may think — but just as fun.
item.dc:creator: ALICIA RANCILIO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pelosi hospitalized in Luxembourg after sustaining injury
item.description: Former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) was hospitalized for evaluation in Luxembourg after sustaining an injury during a trip with a congressional delegation, her spokesperson said Friday. It remains unclear what type of injury Pelosi, 84, sustained.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Duke lacrosse players' rape accuser admits she lied
item.description: A woman who accused three former Duke University men’s lacrosse players of rape nearly two decades ago admitted she lied about the allegations and asked them for forgiveness.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: 5 things to know about Biden’s 1,500 clemencies, pardons
item.description: President Biden pardoned 39 people and commuted the sentences of nearly 1,500 others on Thursday, a sweeping move that comes after pressure from criminal justice advocates over the surprise pardon of his son Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: US updates a science and technology pact with China to reflect growing rivalry and security threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. has updated a decades-old science and technology agreement with China to reflect their growing rivalry for technological dominance.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Flight from LaGuardia diverts to JFK after bird strike: American Airlines
item.description: A flight from LaGuardia Airport to North Carolina was diverted to John F. Kennedy International Airport on Thursday evening due to a reported bird strike, according to American Airlines.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bird strike disables jetliner engine, forces emergency landing at JFK airport
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A bird strike involving an American Airlines jetliner disabled one of the plane's two engines shortly after takeoff from New York's LaGuardia Airport, forcing the flight to turn around and land at John F. Kennedy International Airport, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon to donate $1 million to Trump inauguration, matching Meta
item.description: E-commerce giant Amazon is planning to donate $1 million to President-elect Donald Trump’s inaugural fund, matching the amount Meta already donated to the incoming president’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Americans threaten to take action as drones terrorize multiple states and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online 'constantly': Pew report
item.description: Nearly half of American teenagers say they are online “constantly” despite concerns about the effects of social media and smartphones on their mental health, according to a new report published Thursday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drone mystery: New Jersey homeowners threaten to take matters into their own hands if government doesn't act
item.description: New Jersey residents frustrated by dozens of drone sightings are threatening to take action on their own if the government doesn't start providing answers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some in seafood industry see Trump as fishermen's friend, but tariffs could make for pricier fish
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The incoming administration of President-elect Donald Trump is likely to bring big changes for one of the oldest sectors of the U.S. economy — seafood — and some in the industry believe the returning president will be more responsive to its needs.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey drones: Sheriff calls for state of emergency
item.description: Shaun Golden, the sheriff of Monmouth County, New Jersey, says that more government assistance is needed to identify the drones.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: The wife of a Wisconsin kayaker who faked his own death moves to end their marriage
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The wife of a Wisconsin kayaker who faked his own drowning so he could leave her and their children and meet a woman in Eastern Europe filed court documents Thursday seeking to end their marriage.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Proposed 'Trump' law seeks to allow felons to run for office in Missouri
item.description: A Missouri representative is proposing a repeal of a state law that currently prevents citizens with a felony history from running for a state office.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Used this parking app? You may qualify for part of $32.8 million settlement
item.description: An estimated 21 million users of the ParkMobile parking app are said to have been impacted by the data breach.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Turning dusty attic treasures into cash can yield millions for some and disappointment for others
item.description: THOMASTON, Maine (AP) — Kaja Veilleux has been hunting New England attic treasures for more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kayaker who faked his death, fled the US charged after returning to Wisconsin
item.description: Police had previously said they believed Borgwardt planned his Aug. 12 disappearance and tried to conceal his efforts to mislead investigations.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, ABC 4

item.title: Albertsons sues Kroger for failing to win approval of their proposed supermarket merger
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons' plan for the largest U.S. supermarket merger in history crumbled Wednesday, with Albertsons pulling out of the $24.6 billion deal and the two companies accusing each other of not doing enough to push their proposed alliance through.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Christopher Wray to resign as FBI director ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced Wednesday he plans to resign from the agency, leaving his post ahead of President-Trump's apparent plans to fire him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Albertsons gives up on Kroger merger, sues the grocery chain
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Albertsons pulls the plug on merger with Kroger
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons in 2022 proposed what would be the largest grocery store merger in U.S. history. The companies said a merger would help them better compete with big retailers like Walmart, Costco and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: WATCH: Adorable reaction caught on camera as infant gets new glasses
item.description: An adorable reaction was caught on camera when an infant received a brand new pair of glasses that allowed him to see his parents clearly for the very first time.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP senators say they won’t fight Trump on Jan. 6 pardons
item.description: Republican senators who witnessed firsthand the Jan. 6, 2021, ransacking of congressional offices now say it’s up to President-elect Donald Trump whether to pardon those protesters convicted of federal crimes.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: How to pick the perfect Windows laptop
item.description: Shopping for a new computer can be intimidating. There are so many models to choose from and so many specs to decide on. "They get really excited when they come in here.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione was reported missing 2 weeks before CEO slain: police
item.description: The parents of Luigi Mangione, the man suspected of shooting and killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, told law enforcement that they hadn't heard from their son in months.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Nicole Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect intends to plead not guilty: lawyer
item.description: Alleged UnitedHealthcare CEO shooter Luigi Mangione was arrested at a Pennsylvania McDonald's on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Joe Burrow's home broken into during Monday Night Football
item.description: Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's home was broken into during Monday Night Football in the latest home invasion of a pro athlete in the U.S., authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's in Pennsylvania slammed with bad reviews after arrest of Luigi Mangione
item.description: A McDonald's in Altoona, Pennsylvania, is getting backlash online after an employee called 911, leading to the arrest of Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, KTLA 5

item.title: DA suggests unusual idea for halting Trump’s hush money case while upholding his conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Eager to preserve President-elect Donald Trump ’s hush money conviction even as he returns to office, prosecutors are suggesting various ways forward — including one based on how some courts handle criminal cases when defendants die. Trump’s spokesperson called the ideas “pathetic.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A fugitive gains fame in New Orleans eluding dart guns and nets
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A scruffy little fugitive is on the lam again in New Orleans, gaining fame as he outwits a tenacious band of citizens armed with night-vision binoculars, nets and a tranquilizer rifle.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Customer recalls moment Luigi Mangione spotted in Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: New details are emerging about what happened at the Altoona, Pennsylvania McDonald's where police arrested Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Las Vegas judge dismisses infamous 'Black Widow' murder case after 27 years
item.description: The judge dismissed murder charges against Margaret Rudin, the woman dubbed "The Black Widow" who a jury convicted of killing her husband more than two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: House task force cites 'various failures' ahead of 'preventable' Trump shooting
item.description: The House select committee that investigated the two assassination attempts against President-elect Trump released a report on Tuesday that found the July 13 attack at Trump’s rally in Butler, Pa., was “preventable and should not have happened.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: US wildlife officials plan to label monarch butterflies as threatened species
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service plans to add the butterfly to the threatened species list by the end of next year following an extensive public comment period.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Geminids meteor shower peaks this weekend: How to watch
item.description: People across the globe could see up to 120 meteors per hour during the peak of the Geminids meteor shower this week.
item.dc:creator: Sam Kirk, Fox 5
